title: Leak Sensor Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected water leaking into the base pan.
fullDescription: The AE error code indicates the dishwasher's leak detection sensor has found water in the base pan beneath the tub. LG dishwashers have a float switch in the base that triggers this error when water accumulates where it should not be. The dishwasher will stop operation and activate the drain pump to remove the water. This is a safety feature to prevent water damage to your home.

causes.cause: Loose or damaged water supply connection
Torn or displaced door gasket allowing water to leak
Cracked or leaking spray arm
Faulty tub seal or pump seal
Overfilling due to a stuck water inlet valve

symptoms: AE displayed on the control panel
Dishwasher stops mid-cycle
Water visible under or in front of the dishwasher
Drain pump runs continuously to remove leaked water
Musty smell from standing water in the base

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the water shut-off valve under the kitchen sink that feeds the dishwasher. Turn it clockwise to shut off water. This prevents further water from entering the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out from under the counter (disconnect power first). Inspect the water supply line connection, the drain hose connection, and the area around the door seal for signs of water leakage.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door gasket
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door and examine the rubber door gasket that runs around the tub opening. Look for tears, cracks, gaps, or areas where the gasket has become displaced. Also check for food debris preventing a proper seal.

fixSteps.title: Drain the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the dishwasher back and use towels to soak up water from the base pan. Check if the leak sensor float moves freely. Clean any debris from around the float switch.

fixSteps.title: Run a test cycle
fixSteps.instruction: After fixing the leak source and drying the base pan, reconnect power and water. Run a short cycle and carefully watch for any new leaks from underneath the dishwasher.

whenToCallPro: If the leak is coming from the pump seal, tub, or an internal component that requires significant disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher AE leak error code fix

faq.q: Can the AE error cause water damage?
faq.a: The AE error is actually a protection against water damage. The dishwasher detects the leak and stops operation before significant damage occurs. However, address the cause promptly.

faq.q: How do I reset AE after fixing the leak?
faq.a: Dry the base pan completely and ensure the float switch moves freely. Disconnect power for 5 minutes, then reconnect. The error should clear once the sensor detects no water.

faq.q: Why does AE appear only during certain cycles?
faq.a: Some cycles use more water or higher spray pressure than others, which can reveal leaks that do not appear during gentler cycles.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher is unable to drain water properly.
fullDescription: The OE error code indicates the dishwasher could not drain water within the expected timeframe. Water remains in the tub after the drain cycle should have completed. This can be caused by a clogged drain filter, blocked drain hose, garbage disposal knockout plug not removed, or a faulty drain pump.

causes.cause: Clogged drain filter basket with food debris
Kinked or clogged drain hose
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed (new installation)
Faulty drain pump
Air gap or high loop issue in drain line

symptoms: OE displayed on the control panel
Standing water in the bottom of the dishwasher tub
Dishwasher stops mid-cycle
Dirty water remaining on dishes after cycle
Gurgling sound from the drain area

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door. Remove the lower spray arm and the drain filter assembly at the bottom of the tub. Clean all food debris, grease, and residue from the fine mesh filter and the coarse filter. Rinse under running water.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out and inspect the drain hose from the dishwasher to the garbage disposal or sink drain. Ensure it is not kinked, crushed, or clogged. The drain hose should have a high loop (attached high under the counter) to prevent backflow.

fixSteps.title: Check the garbage disposal connection
fixSteps.instruction: If the dishwasher is connected to a garbage disposal, verify that the knockout plug inside the disposal inlet has been removed (required for new installations). Run the garbage disposal to clear any blockage at the connection point.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the drain pump from underneath the dishwasher. Check for debris blocking the impeller. Test the pump motor for continuity with a multimeter (should read 5-15 ohms). Replace if failed.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor has failed or if the blockage is in the home plumbing rather than the dishwasher.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher OE drain error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: LG recommends cleaning the drain filter after every wash cycle or at least once a week to prevent food debris buildup that causes the OE error.

faq.q: What is the knockout plug on a garbage disposal?
faq.a: When a dishwasher connects to a garbage disposal, there is a plastic plug inside the disposal inlet. This plug must be knocked out during installation to allow water to drain from the dishwasher into the disposal.

faq.q: Why does OE happen only on certain cycles?
faq.a: Some cycles produce more food debris that can temporarily clog the filter or drain. Regular filter cleaning prevents this.

title: Heater Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher's water heating element has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The HE error code indicates the dishwasher's heating element is not able to heat the water to the required temperature within the expected timeframe. Proper water temperature is essential for effective cleaning and sanitizing. The heater may have failed, the water supply temperature may be too low, or the thermostat may be faulty.

causes.cause: Burned out heating element
Incoming water temperature too low (below 120F)
Faulty water temperature sensor (thermistor)
Wiring issue between heater and control board

symptoms: HE displayed on the control panel
Dishes not cleaned properly due to cold water
Cycle times are extended
Sanitize cycle does not pass temperature requirements
Steam is not visible during hot cycles

fixSteps.title: Check incoming water temperature
fixSteps.instruction: Run the hot water at the kitchen sink until it is fully hot. Use a thermometer to verify the temperature is at least 120F (49C). If it is below this, increase your water heater setting.

fixSteps.title: Run hot water before starting
fixSteps.instruction: Before starting the dishwasher, run the kitchen sink hot water for 1-2 minutes until it is as hot as possible. This ensures the dishwasher fills with hot water from the start, reducing the work for the internal heater.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element from underneath the dishwasher. Disconnect the wires and test for continuity with a multimeter. It should read 10-30 ohms. Also test between each terminal and ground. Any reading to ground means the element is shorted.

fixSteps.title: Check the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the water temperature sensor (thermistor) typically mounted on or near the tub sump. Disconnect and test its resistance. At room temperature it should read approximately 50,000 ohms. Replace if readings are abnormal.

whenToCallPro: Heating element replacement requires accessing the bottom of the dishwasher and handling electrical connections. Recommended for most homeowners.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher HE heater error fix

faq.q: What temperature does the dishwasher need?
faq.a: LG recommends incoming hot water of at least 120F (49C). The dishwasher's internal heater boosts this to 140-160F during wash cycles and up to 170F for sanitize cycles.

faq.q: Can cold water cause HE?
faq.a: Yes, if the incoming water is too cold, the heater may not be able to reach the target temperature in time, triggering HE. Run the hot water at your sink before starting the dishwasher.

faq.q: Is HE related to the dry cycle heater?
faq.a: On some models, yes. LG dishwashers may use the same element for wash heating and heated drying. If the element fails, both functions are affected.

title: Thermistor Error
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor (thermistor) has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The tE error code indicates the dishwasher's water temperature sensor (thermistor) is providing abnormal readings or has failed. This sensor monitors water temperature throughout the wash cycle and communicates with the control board to regulate heating. Without accurate temperature data, the dishwasher cannot properly control the wash water temperature.

causes.cause: Defective thermistor sensor
Loose or corroded thermistor wiring connection
Damaged wiring between thermistor and control board
Control board temperature input circuit failure

symptoms: tE displayed on the control panel
Dishwasher will not start or stops mid-cycle
Water temperature not regulated properly
Sanitize function does not work

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Turn it back on and try running a cycle. Transient sensor errors may clear with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Check thermistor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. The thermistor is typically located in the sump area at the bottom of the tub. Check that its wiring connector is firmly seated and free of corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor wiring. Using a multimeter, measure the resistance. At room temperature (77F), it should read approximately 50,000 ohms. If it reads 0 or infinity, replace it.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the faulty thermistor from its mounting (usually a clip or press-fit in the tub or sump). Install the new sensor and reconnect the wiring. Test the dishwasher.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor replacement does not resolve the error, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher tE thermistor error fix

faq.q: Is the thermistor expensive to replace?
faq.a: No, dishwasher thermistors are very affordable at $8-18 for the part. It is one of the simplest and cheapest repairs.

faq.q: What does the thermistor do?
faq.a: The thermistor measures water temperature and sends this data to the control board. The control board uses this information to turn the heating element on and off to maintain the correct wash temperature.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher without fixing tE?
faq.a: The dishwasher may not allow cycles to start with tE active. Even if it does, water temperature will not be regulated, resulting in poor cleaning.

title: Overflow Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected water exceeding the maximum safe level.
fullDescription: The FE error code indicates the dishwasher has detected an overflow condition where water has exceeded the maximum safe level. The dishwasher will automatically attempt to drain excess water. This is a safety feature to prevent water damage. The overflow may be caused by a stuck water inlet valve, faulty water level sensor, or excessive suds.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve stuck open
Excessive detergent causing oversudsing
Faulty water level sensor or float switch
Drain hose siphoning water back into the tub

symptoms: FE displayed on the control panel
Water overflowing from the dishwasher
Dishwasher continuously draining
Excessive foam or suds visible

fixSteps.title: Shut off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately turn off the water supply valve under the kitchen sink to stop water from entering the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Check for excess suds
fixSteps.instruction: If you see excessive foam, you used too much detergent or the wrong type. Only use detergent specifically designed for automatic dishwashers. Run a rinse cycle with no detergent to clear the suds.

fixSteps.title: Verify drain hose installation
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the drain hose has a high loop under the counter (highest point above the flood level of the sink). Without a high loop, water can siphon back into the dishwasher from the sink drain.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the water supply back on with the dishwasher powered off. If water continues to enter the tub when the machine is off, the inlet valve is stuck open and must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve is stuck open, replace it promptly. If you are not comfortable with plumbing connections, call a pro.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher FE overflow error fix

faq.q: Is FE a flood risk?
faq.a: Yes, an overflow condition can cause water damage. Turn off the water supply immediately when you see FE and address the issue before using the dishwasher again.

faq.q: Can dish soap cause FE?
faq.a: Yes, regular dish soap (hand washing soap) creates massive amounts of suds in a dishwasher and will cause an overflow. Only use automatic dishwasher detergent.

faq.q: What type of detergent should I use?
faq.a: Use only detergent labeled for automatic dishwashers. Pods, tablets, powder, or liquid specifically made for dishwashers. Never use hand dish soap.

title: Wash Motor Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher's main wash motor has an electrical malfunction.
fullDescription: The CE error code indicates an electrical fault with the dishwasher's main wash motor. The control board has detected an overcurrent or abnormal electrical condition from the motor that circulates water through the spray arms. This could be a motor failure, wiring issue, or control board problem.

causes.cause: Wash motor winding failure or short circuit
Debris blocking the wash pump impeller
Loose motor wiring connection
Control board motor driver circuit failure

symptoms: CE displayed on the control panel
Dishes not being cleaned (no water spraying)
Humming noise from the wash motor area
Dishwasher stops shortly after filling with water

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 10 minutes. Turn it back on and attempt to run a cycle. A temporary motor overload may clear with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Check for impeller blockage
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm and filter assembly. Check the area around the wash pump inlet for broken glass, food debris, or foreign objects that could jam the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Check motor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the wash motor from underneath the dishwasher. Inspect all wiring connections to the motor for looseness, corrosion, or burn marks. Reconnect any loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Test the wash motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wiring and test the motor windings with a multimeter. It should show 5-10 ohms between the motor terminals. Test for ground faults between each terminal and the motor housing. Replace the motor if readings are abnormal.

whenToCallPro: CE errors involving the wash motor typically require professional diagnosis and repair, especially if the motor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher CE wash motor error fix

faq.q: Is CE serious?
faq.a: CE indicates a motor electrical fault which can worsen if the dishwasher continues to be used. Have it diagnosed promptly.

faq.q: Can broken glass cause CE?
faq.a: Yes, glass fragments can jam the wash pump impeller, causing the motor to stall and draw excessive current, triggering CE.

faq.q: How much does a wash motor cost?
faq.a: LG dishwasher wash motors cost $80-180 for the part. Professional installation adds $100-200 in labor.

title: Motor Locked / Impeller Blocked
shortDescription: The wash motor is unable to rotate due to a mechanical obstruction.
fullDescription: The LE error code indicates the dishwasher's wash motor is mechanically locked and cannot rotate. Unlike CE which is an electrical issue, LE specifically means the motor is physically unable to turn. The most common cause is debris blocking the pump impeller, but it can also indicate bearing failure or a seized motor.

causes.cause: Foreign object blocking the wash pump impeller
Food debris compacted in the pump
Seized pump motor bearings
Broken impeller blade lodged in pump housing

symptoms: LE displayed on the control panel
Motor hums but pump does not circulate water
No water spraying from the spray arms
Grinding noise from the pump area

fixSteps.title: Clean the filter and pump area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack, spray arm, and filter assembly. Clean all food debris from the filter and the area around the pump inlet. Look for broken glass, bones, or other hard objects.

fixSteps.title: Check the pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, reach into the pump inlet area and try to rotate the impeller by hand. It should spin freely. If it is stuck, a foreign object may be lodged in the pump.

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: After clearing any debris, turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Turn it back on and run a quick cycle to test.

fixSteps.title: Access and clear the pump
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the dishwasher from the cabinet to access the pump from underneath. Remove the pump cover or housing to fully clear any debris from the impeller chamber. Check for broken impeller blades.

whenToCallPro: If the motor bearings have seized, the entire motor assembly needs replacement which is best done by a professional.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher LE motor locked impeller fix

faq.q: What commonly blocks the impeller?
faq.a: Broken glass, fruit pits, small bones, toothpicks, and food labels are common culprits. Always scrape dishes before loading and check for small items that could fall through the rack.

faq.q: Can I prevent LE errors?
faq.a: Yes, always scrape food from dishes before loading, regularly clean the filter, and avoid placing items that could break or shed debris.

faq.q: Is LE the same as CE?
faq.a: No. LE is a mechanical blockage (the motor physically cannot turn), while CE is an electrical fault in the motor circuit. The diagnosis and repair differ.

title: Water Inlet Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher is not filling with water properly.
fullDescription: The IE error code indicates the dishwasher did not fill with the required amount of water within the expected timeframe. This could be due to a closed water supply valve, a clogged inlet screen, a faulty water inlet valve, or low household water pressure.

causes.cause: Water supply valve under the sink is closed or partially closed
Clogged inlet screen on the water inlet valve
Faulty water inlet valve
Kinked water supply line

symptoms: IE displayed on the control panel
Dishwasher does not fill with water
Cycle starts but no water enters the tub
Dishwasher times out and displays IE

fixSteps.title: Check the water supply valve
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cabinet under the kitchen sink and locate the water supply valve for the dishwasher (separate from the sink valves). Ensure it is fully open (turned counterclockwise).

fixSteps.title: Inspect the water supply line
fixSteps.instruction: Check the braided supply line from the valve to the dishwasher for kinks or damage. Ensure water flows through it by disconnecting it at the dishwasher end and checking flow into a bucket.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the water supply line from the inlet valve at the bottom of the dishwasher. There is a small mesh screen in the valve inlet. Remove it carefully with needle-nose pliers and clean it under running water.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Test the inlet valve solenoid coil with a multimeter for 500-1500 ohms resistance. If the coil is open, replace the valve assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve needs replacement or if the issue is low household water pressure.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher IE water inlet error fix

faq.q: Is the water supply valve the same as the sink valve?
faq.a: No, the dishwasher has its own dedicated supply valve under the sink, separate from the hot and cold sink valves. It is typically a smaller valve on a branch line.

faq.q: How much water pressure does the dishwasher need?
faq.a: LG dishwashers require a minimum of 20 PSI water pressure and a maximum of 120 PSI.

faq.q: Why does IE happen intermittently?
faq.a: A partially clogged inlet screen can allow enough water sometimes but not always. Clean the screen regularly, especially if you have hard water or sediment in your supply.

title: Motor Triac / Drive Error
shortDescription: The motor drive circuit on the control board has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The bE error code indicates a failure in the motor drive circuit (triac) on the main control board. The triac is a semiconductor device that controls power delivery to the wash motor. When it fails, the motor cannot be properly powered, preventing the dishwasher from washing or draining effectively.

causes.cause: Failed triac (motor drive semiconductor) on control board
Power surge damaging the control board
Motor short circuit causing triac failure
Loose control board connections

symptoms: bE displayed on the control panel
Wash motor does not run
Dishwasher fills but does not wash
Control panel may be unresponsive

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 15 minutes to allow a full reset. Turn it back on and attempt a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check control board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the inner door panel to access the control board. Check all ribbon cables and wiring connectors for loose or corroded connections. Reseat all connections.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the control board for visible burn marks, swollen capacitors, or damaged components, particularly near the motor power output section.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the board shows damage or the bE error persists, replace the main control board. Photograph all connections before disconnecting. Install the new board and reconnect all harnesses.

whenToCallPro: bE typically requires control board replacement which is recommended for professional diagnosis to rule out motor issues first.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher bE motor triac control board fix

faq.q: What is a triac?
faq.a: A triac is a semiconductor switch on the control board that controls power to the wash motor. When it fails, the motor cannot receive proper power.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause bE?
faq.a: Yes, power surges are a common cause of triac failure. Using a surge protector on the dishwasher circuit can help prevent this.

faq.q: Should I replace the motor too?
faq.a: Not necessarily. A technician should test the motor first. If the motor is shorted, it may have caused the triac to fail, and both would need replacement.

title: Leak Detected in Base
shortDescription: Water has been detected in the dishwasher's base pan, triggering the leak protection system.
fullDescription: The E1 error code indicates water has been detected in the base of the dishwasher by the leak protection system. This is functionally similar to the AE error on some models. The dishwasher activates the drain pump to remove water and prevents further operation until the leak is repaired and the base is dry.

causes.cause: Loose hose clamp on an internal water connection
Damaged door gasket allowing spray to escape
Cracked sump or pump housing
Condensation from improper venting or installation

symptoms: E1 displayed on the control panel
Drain pump running continuously
Water under the dishwasher
Dishwasher will not start a new cycle

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Close the dishwasher water supply valve under the sink to prevent further water from entering the machine.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Pull the dishwasher out from the cabinet. Inspect all water connections: supply line, drain hose, internal hose clamps, door gasket, and sump area for signs of water.

fixSteps.title: Dry the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Tip the dishwasher back slightly and absorb all water from the base pan with towels. The leak sensor float must return to its resting position for the error to clear.

fixSteps.title: Fix the leak and test
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten any loose hose clamps, replace damaged gaskets, or repair the identified leak source. Reassemble and run a test cycle while monitoring for new leaks.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot identify the leak source or if it is coming from the sump or pump housing.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher E1 leak error base pan fix

faq.q: Is E1 the same as AE?
faq.a: E1 and AE are similar errors both indicating water in the base pan. Different model series may use different code designations for the same condition.

faq.q: Can overloading cause E1?
faq.a: Indirectly. Overloading with items that redirect spray water toward the door seal can cause water to escape and accumulate in the base.

faq.q: How do I dry the base pan?
faq.a: Carefully tilt the dishwasher back and use towels or a wet-dry vacuum to remove all water. Ensure the float switch moves freely after drying.

title: Network Error (Smart ThinQ)
shortDescription: The dishwasher's Wi-Fi connectivity feature has an error.
fullDescription: The nE error code indicates the dishwasher's Wi-Fi module is experiencing a connectivity issue. This only affects smart features and the LG ThinQ app. The dishwasher operates normally for washing regardless of this error.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi module failed to connect to the network
Home network password or configuration changed
Wi-Fi module hardware failure
Router not broadcasting 2.4 GHz signal

symptoms: nE displayed on the control panel
Cannot connect to the LG ThinQ app
Wi-Fi indicator not lit or flashing
Smart Diagnosis not functioning

fixSteps.title: Reset the Wi-Fi connection
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the Wi-Fi or Smart Diagnosis button for 3 seconds to reset the wireless module.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 2 minutes. Turn it back on and reconnect through the ThinQ app.

fixSteps.title: Check network settings
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your router broadcasts a 2.4 GHz network. The dishwasher cannot connect to 5 GHz networks. Check that the dishwasher is within Wi-Fi range.

fixSteps.title: Re-register the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the dishwasher from the ThinQ app. Factory reset the Wi-Fi module using the button combination in your manual. Then re-add through the app.

whenToCallPro: Only if the Wi-Fi module has physically failed. The dishwasher works normally without Wi-Fi.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher nE WiFi network error fix ThinQ

faq.q: Can I still wash dishes with nE?
faq.a: Yes, nE only affects smart features. All washing functions work normally.

faq.q: Does my dishwasher need Wi-Fi?
faq.a: No, Wi-Fi is optional and only for remote monitoring, cycle notifications, and Smart Diagnosis.

faq.q: Why only 2.4 GHz?
faq.a: LG appliances use 2.4 GHz for better range. If your router uses a combined network, try creating a separate 2.4 GHz SSID.

title: Child Lock Activated
shortDescription: The child lock feature is engaged, preventing operation.
fullDescription: CL is a status indicator showing the child lock is active on the dishwasher. All buttons are disabled to prevent accidental operation. This is a safety feature, not an error code.

causes.cause: Child lock intentionally or accidentally activated
Buttons pressed during panel cleaning
Control panel malfunction
Control board glitch

symptoms: CL displayed on the control panel
No buttons respond
Cannot start or select cycles
Beeping when buttons are pressed

fixSteps.title: Deactivate child lock
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the Child Lock button (or the designated button combination shown on the panel with a lock icon) for 3 seconds until CL disappears.

fixSteps.title: Try alternate button combos
fixSteps.instruction: Common deactivation combos include: Rinse + Spray held for 3 seconds, or Delicate + Dual Zone held for 3 seconds. Check the control panel labels for the lock icon.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: If CL will not deactivate, turn off the circuit breaker for 1 minute. Turn it back on and try deactivating again.

fixSteps.title: Consult the manual
fixSteps.instruction: Look up your specific model's child lock procedure in the user manual or on LG's website for the exact button combination.

whenToCallPro: Only if CL cannot be deactivated after power cycling, suggesting a control panel defect.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher CL child lock deactivate turn off

faq.q: Is CL an error?
faq.a: No, CL is a safety feature. Press and hold the designated buttons for 3 seconds to deactivate.

faq.q: Which buttons turn off CL?
faq.a: It varies by model. Look for a small padlock icon on the control panel near the buttons to hold.

faq.q: Can the dishwasher run with CL?
faq.a: No, child lock prevents all operation. Deactivate it first.

title: Power Failure
shortDescription: The dishwasher experienced a power interruption during a cycle.
fullDescription: The PF code indicates a power failure occurred while the dishwasher was running. This is not a malfunction but an indication that electrical power was interrupted. The dishwasher can resume the cycle after power is restored.

causes.cause: Power outage during operation
Tripped circuit breaker
Loose electrical connection
Faulty home wiring on the circuit

symptoms: PF displayed after power returns
Dishwasher stopped mid-cycle
Display resets or blinks
Cycle does not resume automatically

fixSteps.title: Press Start to resume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Start button to resume the cycle from where it stopped. The dishwasher remembers its position in the cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the dishwasher circuit breaker is fully ON at the electrical panel.

fixSteps.title: Restart if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If you prefer to start fresh, press Cancel/Drain, wait for it to drain, then select a new cycle and press Start.

fixSteps.title: Check for recurring issues
fixSteps.instruction: If PF happens frequently without known outages, have an electrician inspect the circuit and connections.

whenToCallPro: Only if PF occurs repeatedly without known power outages.
videoSearchQuery: LG dishwasher PF power failure error resume

faq.q: Do I need to restart the whole cycle?
faq.a: No, pressing Start resumes from where it stopped. Press Cancel/Drain first if you want to start a fresh cycle.

faq.q: Can PF damage the dishwasher?
faq.a: Occasional power failures will not damage the dishwasher.

faq.q: Should the dishwasher be on a dedicated circuit?
faq.a: Yes, most building codes require dishwashers on a dedicated 15A or 20A circuit.
